description: Chat-based help service for residents to talk with their local city hall — ask about permits, trash pickup, potholes, and council meetings — designed for first-time users who have never used a chat tool before

pages.name: home
pages.description: Welcome landing page that introduces the chat service to residents who have never used it before
components: Top bar with the city seal and service name on the left, a 'Languages' dropdown showing the current language, and a single 'Start a chat' button on the right

Welcome hero block with a friendly headline ('Talk to City Hall'), a two-sentence plain-language explanation of what the service is, and a large 'Ask a question' primary button with a chat-bubble icon
Three-card 'What can I ask about?' row with example topics — each card shows an icon, a topic name (Trash & Recycling, Permits & Licenses, Streets & Parks), and one example question in quotes
Step-by-step 'How it works' strip with four numbered tiles: 1. Pick a topic, 2. Type your question, 3. Chat with a city helper, 4. Get a reference number
Footer with city contact phone number, walk-in office hours, accessibility statement link, and a 'Not an emergency — call 911 for emergencies' notice

animations.element: Large 'Ask a question' primary button with chat-bubble icon inside the welcome hero block

pages.name: chat
pages.description: Main chat window where a resident is having a conversation with a city helper
components: Left sidebar with a 'New question' button at top, a 'Your conversations' heading, a list of past chat threads (each showing topic icon, short title, status pill, and date), and a 'Help' link at the bottom
Top bar of the main pane showing the current chat title, the helper's name and department badge, current wait/response time estimate, and a 'Print this chat' button
Scrollable message thread with alternating bubbles — resident on the right, helper on the left with department avatar and timestamp — plus an inline system intro card and a reference-number card
Suggested-replies row above the composer with three tappable chip buttons (e.g. 'Yes, that helps', 'Can you explain more?', 'I have a follow-up')
Message composer at the bottom with a large plain-language text area, an attachment paperclip with helper text 'Add a photo of the issue', a microphone icon for voice input, and a 'Send' button

animations.element: System intro card 'You are now chatting with Maria from Public Works' at the top of the message thread

pages.name: topics
pages.description: Topic picker page that lets a resident start a new chat by choosing what their question is about
components: Same left sidebar as the chat page
Page heading 'What is your question about?' with a one-sentence subheading 'Pick a topic and we'll connect you with the right department'
Large search input with placeholder 'Type a word like pothole, dog license, or recycling' and a small helper line underneath
Topic grid (three per row) of large tiles — each tile shows a big icon, a department name, a one-line plain-language description, and a small availability status line
'Not sure?' helper card at the bottom with text 'Tell us in your own words and we'll route your question' and a 'Just describe my problem' button

animations.element: Page heading 'What is your question about?' at the top of the main content area

pages.name: report-issue
pages.description: Guided form for reporting a physical issue (pothole, broken streetlight, fallen tree) inside the chat flow
components: Same left sidebar
Stepper bar at the top of the main pane showing four steps — 1. What, 2. Where, 3. Photo, 4. Review — with the current step highlighted and a 'You are on step 2 of 4' plain-language label

Two-column form area — left column has the form fields for the current step (address input, 'Use my current location' button, cross-street field, description text area); right column shows a static map preview with a pin

Reassurance card under the form with three short lines: 'You can stop and come back later', 'Your name stays private unless you choose to share it', and 'You'll get a tracking number when you're done'
Bottom action row with a 'Go back' secondary button on the left and a 'Next step' primary button on the right

animations.element: Stepper bar showing the four steps at the top of the main pane

pages.name: help
pages.description: Beginner-friendly help page explaining how to use the chat service
components: Same left sidebar
Page heading 'How to use this chat' with a friendly one-paragraph welcome aimed at first-time users
Accordion list of six beginner questions — each row has a question on the left and an expand chevron on the right (What is a chat?, Is this free?, Who am I talking to?, and three more)

Side-by-side 'Try it out' demo card showing an example message bubble on the left and a labelled diagram on the right pointing to the composer, send button, and attachment icon with plain-language captions

Contact alternatives card at the bottom listing three other ways to reach the city — phone number with hours, walk-in address with hours, and a paper-form mailing address — each in its own row with an icon

animations.element: Page heading 'How to use this chat' at the top of the main content area
